findingDetails.title: WRITE prompt omits component facts while requiring exact component values
findingDetails.claim: The md-generator WRITE prompt builder cannot reliably satisfy the v3 Components exact-value contract because it never emits component style facts while later requiring exact component values.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked prompt-builder tests and supporting docs for a component facts emission path; tests cover deterministic pre-rendering and focus honesty but not component facts.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A writer could separately inspect tokens.json, but the feature and prompt path claim values come from pre-rendered sections or the FACTS block, which does not hold for Components.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if a documented write workflow always supplies full tokens.components alongside this prompt and tests assert that handoff.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add deterministic component facts or a pre-rendered Components section to build-write-prompt.ts and extend build-write-prompt tests to assert component styles are surfaced verbatim.

findingDetails.title: md-generator output guard does not enforce the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary
findingDetails.claim: The md-generator backend can write generated artifacts outside the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary because it only rejects paths inside the skill directory, then writes artifacts under any other caller-provided output path.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked URL-derived screenshot filenames and found urlToSlug strips separators/non-alphanumeric characters; no URL-controlled traversal was confirmed.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The backend may intentionally allow arbitrary operator-selected output directories, but then the documented spec-folder/sandbox boundary is inaccurate and needs explicit confirmation semantics.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the intended contract is changed to explicitly allow arbitrary operator-selected output paths and command UX adds clear confirmation/rollback language before non-spec writes.

findingDetails.title: Extracted live-site values enter the WRITE prompt without prompt-data isolation
findingDetails.claim: A malicious or compromised source site can inject instruction-like text into the md-generator WRITE prompt through extracted typography values because the prompt builder interpolates live-site-derived strings directly into a natural-language prompt without delimiting or escaping them as untrusted data.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked fidelity cards and hard rules; they prevent hallucinated values but do not isolate live-site strings from prompt instructions or sanitize control/Markdown-like content before the WRITE prompt is consumed.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Most real CSS font-family values are benign and CSS parsing constrains some characters, reducing likelihood but not removing the live-site-to-AI-prompt trust-boundary risk.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if prompt construction serializes all extracted values in a fenced/escaped data-only block, tests cover malicious font/text values, and the writer prompt tells the model to treat extracted content as data only.

findingDetails.title: Report/preview catalog promises overwrite protection the scripts do not implement
findingDetails.claim: The md-generator report/preview feature catalog promises that report-gen, preview-gen, and proof do not silently overwrite existing artifacts, but the scripts write fixed output filenames without checking for existing files or requiring explicit overwrite acceptance.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the three scripts for existsSync, overwrite, force, confirm, and writeFileSync. Existing checks cover inputs/prerequisites only, not output overwrite protection.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The intended behavior may be that specifying the output directory accepts overwrites, but that is not the documented catalog contract.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if docs intentionally state fixed artifacts are overwritten in the selected output directory, or if code adds explicit output-exists/force behavior and coverage.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either implement a shared output-exists guard or revise the catalog and playbook to state overwrite semantics honestly.

findingDetails.title: Procedure-card schema lint is manual-only and outside the existing canon checker
findingDetails.claim: The private procedure-card schema is conforming today, but the required-field lint can be silently skipped on the next card edit because it is documented as a manual pre-publish check and the existing canon checker still has no procedures coverage.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the schema, Phase 007 verification records, and sampled cards for a current conformance failure; none was found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Manual lint may be an intentional lightweight process for private mode guidance, but it remains a future drift risk.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if a runnable checker or validation rule already exists and is documented as required for every procedure-card edit.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a small procedure-card lint check to the existing canon/validation path or document a required validation command for every card edit.

findingDetails.title: Byte-identical benchmark artifacts remain under two incompatible naming conventions
findingDetails.claim: The same benchmark evidence remains under both after-012-routing-rigor/report and after-d3-proxy/skill-benchmark-report names, which can be mistaken for separate runs.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked Phase 012 docs for visibility; the duplicate is clearly documented, so this is not a false-completion defect.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Leaving both artifacts was correct for the Phase 012 doc-only reconciliation scope; cleanup belongs to a future phase.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if a future cleanup consolidates the duplicate or marks after-d3-proxy as a deprecated alias in benchmark documentation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Consolidate to one canonical folder/name or add a benchmark README note that marks after-d3-proxy as an orphaned duplicate.

findingDetails.title: Guided run validates relative output against one cwd but executes extraction from another
findingDetails.claim: The guided wrapper accepts and preflights the documented repo-root relative output path, but extraction runs from BACKEND_ROOT with the original relative path, so extract.ts resolves the same argument inside the skill/backend tree and rejects it as unsafe.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked guided-run tests for path-normalization or execution coverage. Tests cover parsing, plan labels, and unsafe skill path only; they do not assert absolute/caller-normalized extract args or execute the cwd-sensitive plan.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Operators can supply an absolute output path, but that workaround does not satisfy the documented repo-root relative workflow and does not fix the inconsistent path model inside the wrapper.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if guided-run is intentionally changed to require absolute output paths only and tests/docs reject repo-root relative paths.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Focused reads of guided-run, extract, backend README, CLI comments, and guided-run tests show the relative path is accepted, planned unchanged, executed from BACKEND_ROOT, and only tested as a string plan.
findingDetails.recommendation: Resolve output once from the operator cwd, validate that resolved path, pass the resolved path to spawned commands, and use it for wrapper writes, validation, report, and tests.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Spec-folder review spans a 13-phase packet plus sk-design mode packets and md-generator backend scripts; this iteration is a deep revisit over a high-risk mutating backend surface.
